repair_career-decision_R2_5.text:

node relocation_cost "Relocation: new language, no network, uprooting" [role assumption]

node optimization_tension "Core question: optimize for comfort or growth?" [role question]
relation berlin_offer -> cost_of_living offsets [label "salary gain may cancel out"]
relation berlin_offer -> remote_loss tradeoff [sign -]
relation remote_loss -> comfort tradeoff [sign -]
relation relocation_cost -> growth_potential tradeoff [sign +] [label "discomfort as catalyst"]
relation current_job -> comfort reinforces [sign +]
relation current_job -> optimization_tension contradicts [label "stability vs. stagnation"]
relation berlin_offer -> optimization_tension supports [label "growth opportunity"]
relation optimization_tension -> claim precedes [as inference]

place current_job inside tradeoffs
place remote_loss inside tradeoffs
place relocation_cost inside tradeoffs
place growth_potential inside tradeoffs

repair_gamblers-fallacy_R1_2.text:
atomik 0.3
scene gamblers_fallacy
claim "The probability of black on the next roulette spin is unaffected by previous results." [status established]
subject [[Gambler's fallacy]]
node intuition "Black is due after five reds" [status misconception] [role assumption]
node independence "Each spin is independent" [role evidence]
node probability "P(black) ≈ 48.6% on any spin" [role evidence]
node memory "The wheel has no memory" [role assumption]
relation intuition -> probability refutes [as refutation]
relation independence -> intuition refutes [as refutation]
input prediction = choice "Black is more likely now" | "Still 48.6%" [label "After five reds in a row, what's the probability of black?"]
step 1 note "Five reds in a row just happened. It feels like black should come next..."
step 1 reveal intuition
step 2 require prediction
step 3 reveal memory
step 3 reveal independence
step 4 reveal probability
step 4 highlight probability
step 5 note "The fallacy: we mistake pattern-seeking for causation. The wheel doesn't know or 'correct' itself."

repair_gamblers-fallacy_R1_5.text:
atomik 0.3
scene gamblers_fallacy
claim "The gambler's fallacy—belief that past outcomes affect independent future probabilities—is a cognitive error." [status established]
subject [[Gambler's fallacy]]

node fallacy "Black is 'due' after five reds" [status misconception]
node intuition "Strong intuition that patterns must reverse" [role assumption]
node independence "Each spin is independent" [role evidence]
node memory "The wheel has no memory" [role evidence]
node truth "Probability of black remains 48.6%, always"

evidence indep "In independent trials, prior outcomes don't change future probabilities" [status established]
evidence wheel_phys "A roulette wheel's next spin depends only on its physical state, not history" [status established]

input predict = choice "Black is due (overdue)" | "Red is due (hot hand)" | "Probability unchanged" [label "After five reds in a row, what happens next?"]

relation fallacy -> indep refutes [as refutation]
relation wheel_phys -> fallacy refutes [as refutation]
relation memory -> fallacy refutes [as fact]
relation indep -> truth supports [as fact]

step 1 note "A roulette wheel just landed red five times in a row."
step 1 reveal fallacy, intuition
step 2 require predict
step 2 note "Now let's check the facts."
step 3 reveal independence, memory
step 3 highlight truth
step 4 reveal wheel_phys, indep
step 4 note "The wheel has no memory. Probability is constant."

project as tree [suggested]

claim "The defining features of mammals are milk production and warm-bloodedness, not habitat or reproductive mode." [status established]

group criterial_features "Criterial features" kind cluster
group incidental_features "Incidental features" kind cluster
node milk "Produce milk for young" [in criterial_features] [salience criterial]
node warm_blooded "Warm-blooded" [in criterial_features] [salience criterial]
node habitat "Habitat (aquatic, terrestrial, aerial)" [in incidental_features] [salience incidental]
node egg_laying "Reproductive mode (egg-laying vs live birth)" [in incidental_features] [salience incidental]
node bat "Bat"
node whale "Whale"
node platypus "Platypus"
node shark "Shark"
evidence e_shark "Shark swims like a whale" [status established]
relation milk -> claim supports [as fact] [sign +]
relation warm_blooded -> claim supports [as fact] [sign +]
relation habitat -> claim contradicts [as fact] [sign -] [label "does not determine"]
relation egg_laying -> claim contradicts [as fact] [sign -] [label "does not determine"]
relation bat -> milk supports [as fact]
relation whale -> milk supports [as fact]
relation platypus -> milk supports [as fact]
relation bat -> warm_blooded supports [as fact]
relation whale -> warm_blooded supports [as fact]
relation platypus -> warm_blooded supports [as fact]
relation e_shark -> claim refutes [as fact]
project as graph [suggested]
